perturbation.original_text: 3. All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the disclosing Party and nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring any rights to such Confidential Information on the other Party. The Recipient shall honor any request from the disclosing Party to promptly return or destroy all copies of Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement and all notes related to such Confidential Information. The Parties agree that the disclosing Party will suffer irreparable injury if its Confidential Information is made public, released to a third party, or otherwise disclosed in breach of this Agreement and that the disclosing Party shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against a threatened breach or continuation of any such breach and, in the event of such breach, an award of actual and exemplary damages from any court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the disclosing Party and nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring any rights to such Confidential Information on the other Party. The Recipient may or may not honor any request from the disclosing Party to promptly return or destroy all copies of Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement and all notes related to such Confidential Information. The Parties agree that the disclosing Party will suffer irreparable injury if its Confidential Information is made public, released to a third party, or otherwise disclosed in breach of this Agreement and that the disclosing Party shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against a threatened breach or continuation of any such breach and, in the event of such breach, an award of actual and exemplary damages from any court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall honor' to 'may or may not honor', this creates a contradiction. While the agreement states Confidential Information remains the property of the disclosing party, the recipient is no longer unequivocally obligated to return or destroy it, which undermines the original intent to protect the information. This contradicts the property rights provision.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. All Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the disclosing Party and nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring any rights to such Confidential Information on the other Party. The Recipient may or may not honor any request from the disclosing Party to promptly return or destroy all copies of Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement and all notes related to such Confidential Information. The Parties agree that the disclosing Party will suffer irreparable injury if its Confidential Information is made public, released to a third party, or otherwise disclosed in breach of this Agreement and that the disclosing Party shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against a threatened breach or continuation of any such breach and, in the event of such breach, an award of actual and exemplary damages from any court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the recipient 'shall honor' requests to return or destroy confidential information. The changed text modifies this to 'may or may not honor', directly contradicting the initial obligation. This creates a clear conflict with the intention of protecting the disclosing party's property rights as previously established in the agreement itself, which is also the 'Contradicted Text', making the protection of confidential information ambiguous and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: 4. The terms of this Agreement shall not be construed to limit either Party’s right to develop independently or acquire products without use of the other Party’s Confidential Information. The disclosing party acknowledges that the Recipient may currently or in the future be developing information internally, or receiving information from other parties, that is similar to the Confidential Information. Nothing in this Agreement will prohibit the Recipient from developing or having developed for it products, concepts, systems or techniques that are similar to or compete with the products, concepts, systems or techniques contemplated by or embodied in the Confidential Information Non-Disclosure Agreement PRIVATE AND CONFIDENTIAL 2 provided that the Recipient does not violate any of its obligations under this Agreement in connection with such development.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. The terms of this Agreement shall be construed to limit either Party’s right to develop independently or acquire products even if the Recipient has access to the other Party’s Confidential Information. The disclosing party acknowledges that the Recipient may currently or in the future be developing information internally, or receiving information from other parties, that is similar to the Confidential Information. Nothing in this Agreement will prohibit the Recipient from developing or having developed for it products, concepts, systems or techniques that are similar to or compete with the products, concepts, systems or techniques contemplated by or embodied in the Confidential Information Non-Disclosure Agreement PRIVATE AND CONFIDENTIAL 2 provided that the Recipient does not violate any of its obligations under this Agreement in connection with such development.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "shall not be construed to limit" to "shall be construed to limit", this creates a contradiction. While the agreement states Confidential Information remains the property of the disclosing party. the recipient can now acquire products while in possession of the other party's confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. The terms of this Agreement shall be construed to limit either Party’s right to develop independently or acquire products even if the Recipient has access to the other Party’s Confidential Information. The disclosing party acknowledges that the Recipient may currently or in the future be developing information internally, or receiving information from other parties, that is similar to the Confidential Information. Nothing in this Agreement will prohibit the Recipient from developing or having developed for it products, concepts, systems or techniques that are similar to or compete with the products, concepts, systems or techniques contemplated by or embodied in the Confidential Information Non-Disclosure Agreement PRIVATE AND CONFIDENTIAL 2 provided that the Recipient does not violate any of its obligations under this Agreement in connection with such development.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement 'shall not be construed to limit' either party's right to develop independently. The changed text alters this to 'shall be construed to limit', directly contradicting the original intent and the rest of the clause which suggests the Recipient can develop similar products. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text. This is a direct contradiction introduced by the modification.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Neither Party will, without prior approval of the other Party, make any public announcement of or otherwise disclose the existence or the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Either Party can, without approval of the other Party, make any public announcement of or otherwise disclose the existence or the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By negating the requirement for prior approval before public announcement, a direct contradiction is introduced. The original agreement strictly prohibits disclosure without consent, while the modified agreement explicitly allows it, which undermines the confidential nature intended by the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Either Party can, without approval of the other Party, make any public announcement of or otherwise disclose the existence or the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text strictly prohibits either party from making public announcements without prior approval, while the changed text explicitly allows either party to make such announcements without approval. This creates a direct and undeniable contradiction. The contradicted text and changed text are the same, thus it creates a contradiction to the initial text because the changed text allows for something that previously was not allowed.
